spec_match.rationale: The lab probes the actual UIEvent, AnimationEvent, TransitionEvent, Element.pseudo(), and CSSPseudoElement surfaces, and the conformance suite now fails honestly when those contracts are absent.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the card click, animation button, transition button, forced fallback toggle, coordinate pseudo-region fallback, and clear button.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now demonstrates all event classes promised by the feature blurb and shows the unsupported-browser fallback path when pseudoTarget is missing.

edge_cases.rationale: The forced fallback mode and geometry branch let visitors inspect the no-pseudoTarget path even in browsers that later expose the API.

references.rationale: The concept includes chromestatus.com/feature/5179328935624704 plus CSSOM and MDN references.

design_system.rationale: A current static scan found no raw hex colors, inline styles, or inline handlers, and all local button background overrides pair an explicit text color.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148 at /v147/pseudo-target-on-events/compatibility-lab/?verify=pseudo-target-fix. The page loaded with all API probes showing unsupported, the ChromeStatus link present, and a clean fallback state. Tested center card click, Animate, Transition, Force fallback, coordinate click inside the visible ::before region, and Clear. The log showed the fallback body click, animationend with beforeSpin, transitionend for opacity, forced fallback mode, inferred ::before region, and reset. Concept and conformance routes had no console messages and successful network requests. The updated conformance route showed 0 PASS / 7 FAIL in Chrome 148, matching the browser's current lack of the claimed pseudoTarget/CSSPseudoElement contracts instead of falsely passing.
